provenance.title: Asynchronous Deep Double Duelling Q-Learning for Trading-Signal Execution in Limit Order Book Markets

authors.name: Peer Nagy
authors.affiliation: Oxford-Man Institute of Quantitative Finance, University of Oxford

authors.name: Jan-Peter Calliess
authors.affiliation: Oxford-Man Institute of Quantitative Finance, University of Oxford

authors.name: Stefan Zohren
authors.affiliation: Oxford-Man Institute of Quantitative Finance, University of Oxford

provenance.abstract: We employ deep reinforcement learning (RL) to train an agent to successfully translate a high-frequency trading signal into a trading strategy that places individual limit orders. Based on the ABIDES limit order book simulator, we build a reinforcement learning OpenAI gym environment and utilise it to simulate a realistic trading environment for NASDAQ equities based on historic order book messages.
key_claims: RL agent successfully translates a noisy directional high-frequency signal into a limit order trading strategy
Deep Duelling Double Q-learning with APEX architecture outperforms a heuristic baseline on both mean return and Sharpe ratio
RL outperforms baseline for all three noise levels investigated (a=1.1, 1.3, 1.6)
First study applying the APEX algorithm to limit order book environments
RL agent adapts aggression level (bid/mid/ask placement) based on signal quality — more passive with better signals to reduce transaction costs

architecture.description: Deep Duelling Double DQN with APEX (asynchronous prioritised experience replay, used uniformly). Q-network has feed-forward layers followed by an LSTM layer, split into value-stream and advantage-stream (duelling). A main network and a target network are maintained.

components.name: SharedFeatureEncoder
components.description: 3 feed-forward (fully-connected) layers followed by 1 LSTM layer. Shared trunk before the duelling split.
components.notes: Number of FF layers explicitly stated as 3. Hidden dims not explicitly stated.

components.name: AdvantageStream
components.description: Estimates A(s,a) — advantage of each action over mean.

components.name: QNetwork
components.description: Combines value and advantage streams: Q(s,a) = V(s) + A(s,a) - mean(A(s,.)). Instantiated twice as main_network and target_network.

components.name: APEXTrainingFramework
components.description: 42 parallel CPU workers sample experience asynchronously into a shared replay buffer (size 2e6). A single GPU learner samples uniformly (prioritised replay disabled) and updates the Q-network. After target_network_update_freq steps (5000), target network is updated. Policy broadcast back to workers.

action_space.description: 7 discrete actions: {sell@bid, sell@mid, sell@ask, buy@bid, buy@mid, buy@ask, skip}
action_space.encoding: at in ({-1,1} x {-1,0,1}) union {skip}; direction x price_level

state_space.description: History of LOB observations of length l=100, including: time remaining (T-t), cash balance, stock inventory, directional signal (3-class prob vector), best bid/ask price+volume+agent's own resting volume. All quantities for current step and l previous steps.

equations.name: Synthetic Directional Signal
equations.latex: d_t = \phi d_{t-1} + (1-\phi)\epsilon_t, \quad \epsilon_t \sim \text{Dirichlet}(\alpha(r_{t+h})), \quad r_{t+h} = \frac{p_{t+h} - p_t}{p_t}, \quad p_{t+h} = \frac{1}{h}\sum_{i=1}^{h} p_{t+i}

equations.name: Dirichlet Parameter Assignment
equations.latex: \alpha(r_{t+h}) = \begin{cases} (a^H, a^L, a^L) & \text{if } r_{t+h} < -k \\ (a^L, a^H, a^L) & \text{if } -k \leq r_{t+h} < k \\ (a^L, a^L, a^H) & \text{if } k \leq r_{t+h} \end{cases}

equations.name: Mark-to-Market Portfolio Value
equations.latex: M_t = C_t + X_t p^m_t, \quad \Delta M_t = \Delta C_t + X_{t-1}\Delta p^m_t + \Delta X_t p^m_t

equations.name: Total Reward
equations.latex: r_{t+1} = w^{dir} R^{dir}_{t+1} + (1 - w^{dir}) R^{pnl}_{t+1}, \quad w^{dir} \leftarrow \psi w^{dir}

tensor_semantics.notes: hidden_dim not explicitly stated; assumed 256 based on common practice for similar RL architectures.

training_pipeline.algorithm: Deep Double Duelling Q-learning with APEX
training_pipeline.episode_duration: 1 hour (09:30–10:30, one trading day per episode)

reward_directional_weight_initial.basis: common curriculum RL practice; decayed by psi each step

reward_psi_decay.basis: must decay from initial w_dir to ~0 over 300M steps

reward_kappa.basis: scale factor; set to 1.0 pending paper clarification

baselines.name: Heuristic Baseline
baselines.description: Crosses spread aggressively when signal indicates directional move; places passive order to reduce position when signal is neutral. Uses same action space and signal as RL agent.

baselines.name: Buy & Hold
baselines.description: Passive buy-and-hold over the same test period.

evaluation_protocol.statistical_testing: t-test across 31 episodes; p << 0.1 for all noise levels

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Hidden dimension of FF layers and LSTM set to 256
implementation_assumptions.basis: common practice for similar DQN architectures in finance RL

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Initial directional reward weight w_dir = 0.5, decayed by psi = 0.9999 per step
implementation_assumptions.basis: paper states it decays from initial weight toward 0; values inferred from needing to reach near-zero over ~300M steps

implementation_assumptions.assumption: LOBSTER dataset requires institutional purchase; synthetic or public AAPL order flow used for testing
implementation_assumptions.basis: LOBSTER is a commercial dataset (Huang & Polak 2011)

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Adam optimizer used (RLlib default with APEX)
implementation_assumptions.basis: RLlib APEX default; paper does not contradict

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Inventory constraint enforcement: if action would exceed [posmin, posmax], action is masked (disallowed) before selection
implementation_assumptions.basis: paper states: 'Actions which would immediately result in positions outside the allowed inventory constraints are disallowed'

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Market order triggered when a passive fill would take inventory outside bounds

ambiguities.description: Hidden dimension of FF layers not stated. Only '3 feed-forward layers followed by an LSTM layer' mentioned.
Initial value of w_dir and decay rate psi not given numerically.
kappa (scale of directional reward) not numerically specified.
Exact state_dim per timestep not given as a number; reconstructed as 10 from listing of state components.
